comment.body: Thanks for the feedback. You're right — my current approach is rule-based and works only for straightforward expressions. A more general implementation would expose the calculator as a tool to the model and let the model decide when to use it and what expression to pass. I'll refine it in that direction.

Thanks, that makes sense. My current implementation is rule-based for simplicity, but a more general agent design would delegate tool selection to the model itself through prompting. That would make it more flexible for natural language queries where the mathematical expression is not explicitly structured.

issue.title: Orders are saved with a hardcoded date.
issue.body:
### Description
In `coffeeMain.cpp`, the `orderCoffee()` function saves every order with a fixed date string: `orderDates[orderCount] = "2025-05-26"`.

### Expected Behavior
Orders should reflect the actual current date of the transaction.

### Suggested Fix
Use the `<ctime>` or `<chrono>` library to fetch the system date at the time of order placement.

comment.body: Added the getCurrentDate() function using the <ctime> library. It will allow system to fetch the current data automatically

issue.title: Orders are saved with a hardcoded date.
issue.body:
### Description
In `coffeeMain.cpp`, the `orderCoffee()` function saves every order with a fixed date string: `orderDates[orderCount] = "2025-05-26"`.

### Expected Behavior
Orders should reflect the actual current date of the transaction.

### Suggested Fix
Use the `<ctime>` or `<chrono>` library to fetch the system date at the time of order placement.
